summary: PIN Login Bug Fix - All Tests PASSED. Verified short PIN login (1-8 digits) works correctly after the fix to Login.js line 163 (changed from pin.length < 4 to pin.length < 1).
passed_tests: Backend: Login with PIN '100' returns 200 OK with valid token
Backend: Login with PIN '123' returns 200 OK with valid token
Backend: PIN update via PUT /api/users/{id} works correctly
Frontend: ENTRAR button is initially disabled (no PIN entered)
Frontend: ENTRAR button enables with 1 digit PIN (pin.length < 1 check)
Frontend: ENTRAR button enables with PIN '100' (3 digits)
Frontend: Login with PIN '100' navigates to /dashboard successfully
Frontend: User edit dialog opens when clicking Admin user
Frontend: PIN change to '123' shows success toast 'Usuario actualizado exitosamente'
Frontend: Login with changed PIN '123' navigates to /dashboard successfully
Full Flow: Login -> Settings -> Change PIN to '123' -> Logout -> Login with '123' PASSED
critical_code_review_comments: Login.js line 29: Correctly allows PIN length >= 1 with `if (pin.length < 1) return;`
Login.js line 163: Button disabled condition correctly set to `pin.length < 1 || loading`
auth.py lines 151-155: Backend PIN validation correctly allows 1-8 digits, rejects leading zeros
auth.py line 170: Login endpoint correctly hashes PIN and queries database
seed_data_creation: Admin PIN restored to '100' after testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: PIN login bug is FIXED. Admin PIN is '100'. The fix was in Login.js line 163 where the disabled condition was changed from `pin.length < 4` to `pin.length < 1`. Short PINs (1-8 digits) now work correctly for both login and PIN changes.

features_verified.button_enable_1_digit: PASS - ENTRAR button enables after 1 digit
features_verified.pin_change_flow: PASS - Change PIN in Settings works
features_verified.logout_login_new_pin: PASS - Can login with newly changed PIN

bug_description: Users could not login after changing PIN to short value (e.g., '100') because ENTRAR button was disabled for PINs < 4 digits
